series_metadata.series_description: Original application and subsequent waiver forms submitted to Washington during the Reagan Administration that led to reform of the welfare system, changing the way services were provided and monies were allocated to eligible recipients under public assistance programs. Reform helped make receipt of benefits more conditional, based on behavioral changes and looking for work, rather than just allocating monies. File includes summaries of what was proposed for Ohio recipients, and evaluation reports.

retention_rules.trigger_event: Electronic: in office , review by State Archives prior destruction
